question.text: Which sections of the SEBI Act, 1992 were violated by FFSL and its connected entities as per the case study?

answer.text: Section 30(a), (b) and (c) of the SEBI Act
Section 11(1), 11(2) and 11(3) of the SEBI Act
Section 12A(a), (b) and (c) of the SEBI Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FFSL and its connected entities violated provisions of Section 12A(a), (b) and (c) of the SEBI Act, 1992 read with Regulations 3(a), (b), (c), (d) and Regulations 4(1), 4(2)(a) and (e) of PFUTP Regulations. **The Trap:** Section 15HA deals with penalties, not the substantive prohibition. Section 11 deals with SEBI's powers, not prohibition of fraud. **Cross-Exam Context:** Section 12A is the backbone provision for fraud prohibition and is tested across NISM Series III-A, VIII, and IX exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 12A prohibits fraud; Section 15HA prescribes penalties - keep these distinct in your preparation.
